name: Nigeria

visa_free.nationality: ECOWAS member states (Benin, Cabo Verde, Côte d'Ivoire, The Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo)
visa_free.notes: Citizens of ECOWAS member states are exempt from the standard visa requirement. The NIS Tourism Visa (F5A) page explicitly states it applies to 'Citizens of all countries except ECOWAS member States and other countries which Nigeria has entered into visa abolition/waiver agreements'. As of June 2026 there are 12 active ECOWAS members (15 minus Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger). Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger formally withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025, after the one-year statutory notice period elapsed. ECOWAS granted a six-month transition period (January 29 – July 29, 2025) during which visa-free movement for their nationals was explicitly maintained. After the transition period ended July 29, 2025, ECOWAS has not announced formal termination of visa-free movement; it continues 'until further notice' per ECOWAS Commission statements. The official NIS Tourism Visa (F5A) page and immigration.gov.ng do not specify whether Burkina Faso, Mali, or Niger nationals retain visa-free access to Nigeria following the formal ECOWAS withdrawal. Their visa-free status is recorded in data_quality.gaps. Max stay under the free movement protocol is governed by the ECOWAS Protocol and is not stated verbatim on the NIS website.

visa_free.nationality: Countries with which Nigeria has entered into visa abolition/waiver agreements (non-ECOWAS)
visa_free.notes: The official NIS Tourism Visa (F5A) page states the visa exemption also applies to 'other countries which Nigeria has entered into visa abolition/waiver agreements'. The Ministry of Interior visa policy page confirms that 'citizens of countries with which Nigeria has visa agreements are being issued entry visa at points of entry'. The specific list of named bilateral waiver countries for ordinary passport holders is not published in a consolidated official online source on immigration.gov.ng or interior.gov.ng.

Reciprocal 90-day visa-free entry agreement effective 4 August 2025. Confirmed by Nigeria Immigration Service spokesperson Akinsola Akinlabi (cited by TheCable). High-level agreement follows engagements between President Tinubu and PM Dr. Terrance M. Drew. The official SKNIS announcement (sknis.gov.kn) and SKN Consulate Toronto announcement (skncgtoronto.gov.kn) confirm the effective date and reciprocal nature. Specific passport types (ordinary/official/diplomatic) not delineated in official announcement text; 90-day duration confirmed by NIS spokesperson.

30-day visa-free entry for Rwandan nationals announced by President Bola Tinubu at the Africa CEO Forum in Kigali, Rwanda (May 2026), reciprocating Rwanda's existing visa waiver for Nigerian citizens. The Nigeria Immigration Service, through its Public Relations Officer Akinsola Akinlabi, confirmed on approximately May 15, 2026 that all arrangements had been completed for implementation at all international entry points (airports, land borders, seaports). Those wishing to stay beyond 30 days must obtain appropriate visas via Nigerian embassies/high commissions or the e-Visa portal. Specific passport types (ordinary/official/diplomatic) not delineated in the official NIS statement; context suggests it applies to all ordinary Rwandan nationals. No dedicated immigration.gov.ng page found for this agreement at time of June 2026 verification; source is credible Nigerian media (Telegraph Nigeria, Vanguard, ThisDay) citing the official NIS spokesman statement.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not covered by visa-free/waiver exemptions
e_visa.notes: Nigeria offers an e-Visa for short visits via evisa.immigration.gov.ng. The NIS visa classes page states 'Short Visit Visas allow travelers to visit Nigeria for a period not exceeding three months (90 days)'. Individual sub-categories have specific durations: Tourism Visa (F5A) – single entry, 30-day stay, valid 90 days from issuance, not extendable; Business Single Entry (F4A) – 30 days; Business Multiple Entry (F4B) – up to 30 days per visit; Visiting Single Entry (F6A) – 30 days; Transit Visa (F3B) – 1 to 7 days; Sports (F7E), Creative Arts (F7F), Study Tour (F7G), Academic Exchange Programme (F7H), International Cultural Exchange (F7I), Emergency/Relief Work (F7K), and others. The maximum ceiling across all short-stay e-visa categories is 90 days; most specific tourism/business categories are capped at 30 days per visit. All short-stay e-Visa categories are non-extendable. Nigeria phased out Visa-on-Arrival effective 1 May 2025; all short-stay applications must now be submitted via the e-Visa portal or Nigerian embassy/consulate.

visa_policy.notes: Nigeria phased out Visa-on-Arrival (VoA) effective 1 May 2025. All short-stay visa applications must now be submitted via the e-Visa portal (evisa.immigration.gov.ng) or at Nigerian embassies/consulates. Transit Without Visa (F3A) is available to airside transit passengers who remain in the airport transit zone for up to 48 hours with a confirmed onward ticket - no formal visa is issued. The Ministry of Interior visa policy page notes that notable businessmen (frequent travelers) and top management of multinationals may be issued entry visas at points of entry, and that citizens of visa-agreement countries may also receive entry visas at points of entry. The general NIS short-visit-visa page states the ceiling as 90 days; most individual e-visa sub-categories (tourism, visiting, business single entry) are limited to 30 days per stay.

conditional_access.conditions: Bilateral Agreement on the Waiver of Visa requirements for holders of Diplomatic and Official/Service Passports signed 15 February 2026 on the margins of the 39th Ordinary Session of the African Union in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Signed by Nigeria's Minister of Foreign Affairs Ambassador Yusuf Maitama Tuggar and Angola's Minister of External Relations Ambassador Tete Antonio. The foreignaffairs.gov.ng page confirming this had an SSL certificate issue at time of re-validation (June 2026); the agreement is confirmed by the official MFA press center URL and corroborated by Voice of Nigeria (state broadcaster, von.gov.ng).

Agreement on waiver of visa requirements for holders of valid Diplomatic and Official Passports signed 30 October 2019 at Presidential Villa, Abuja (witnessed by Vice President Yemi Osinbajo and Vietnamese Deputy PM Vuong Dinh Hue). Effective 29 November 2019. Holders may enter, exit, and transit Nigeria without a visa for a maximum of 90 days from date of entry. No standalone official Nigerian MFA/NIS press-release page was accessible at time of research; source is Channels Television (state-accredited Nigerian broadcaster reporting the signing ceremony at the Presidential Villa).

MOU on waiver of visa requirements for holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports signed February 2020 in Addis Ababa in the presence of President Muhammadu Buhari and Ethiopian PM Abiy Ahmed. Signed by Nigeria's Minister of Foreign Affairs Geoffrey Onyeama and Ethiopian counterpart Gedu Andargachew. A subsequent ratification by Ethiopia's cabinet was reported in July 2025. No standalone official Nigerian MFA/NIS press-release page was accessible at time of research; source is TheCable, a credible Nigerian news outlet reporting the official signing ceremony.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Nigerian government domain. Nigeria's investor immigration pathway (Investor Visa N3A–N3E) leads to Permanent Residence, not citizenship. No official CBI program has been launched on immigration.gov.ng or any other official government domain as of June 2026.

programs.name: Investor Visa – Small Scale Enterprise (N3A)
programs.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV) for small-scale enterprise investors. Requires evidence of US$250,000 – US$500,000 imported and retained as investor's capital, prior holding of F4B or F4C visa, security clearance from place of residence, proof of investment in a small enterprise, and business plan. Stay duration: 3 years, multiple entry (residence permit). Not applicable for extension, but residency can be renewed subject to continued compliance.

programs.name: Investor Visa – Medium Scale Enterprise (N3B)
programs.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV) for medium-scale enterprise investors. Requires evidence of US$500,000 – US$1,000,000 imported and retained as investor's capital, prior F4B or F4C visa holding, security clearance. Stay duration: 4 years, multiple entry (residence permit).

programs.name: Investor Visa – Large Scale Corporations (N3C)
programs.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV) for large-scale corporation investors. Minimum capital threshold is not stated as a verbatim dollar amount on the accessible official page. Requires significant capital/asset requirements verified by corporate registration and audited financial statements. Residency granted to key executives.

programs.name: Investor Visa – Ultra Large Scale Corporations (N3D)
programs.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV) for investors leading major multinational or ultra-large corporations. Must meet the highest thresholds of capital, assets, or turnover (specific amounts not stated verbatim on official page). Multi-year residency for strategic oversight.

programs.name: Investor Visa – Oil, Gas & Power Sector (N3E)
programs.notes: Specialized Permanent Residence Visa (PRV) for significant ventures in Nigeria's oil, gas, or power sectors. Requires technical expertise, substantial capital, and relevant industry licenses or partnerships. Minimum capital not stated verbatim on official page.

programs.name: Retirement Visa – Retiree from Abroad (N5B)
programs.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV) for foreign retirees wishing to relocate to Nigeria from overseas. Applicants must demonstrate sufficient financial resources (pensions, annuities, savings) and secure local accommodation. Health insurance strongly recommended. Employment not permitted. Specific minimum income/savings threshold not stated verbatim on official page.

programs.name: Highly Skilled Immigrant Visa (N4A)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals with specialized knowledge, abilities, and technical know-how deemed highly relevant to current and future needs of the Nigerian economy. Requires letter of recommendation from the Nigerian Skills Gap Assessment (under ITF), vetted credentials and CV/skill profile, and police report from place of residence.
programs.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV) category. Stay duration: 5 years, multiple entry (residence permit), extendable. Holder is permitted to work and reside in Nigeria. Falls under the Nigerian Immigration Service's Permanent Residence framework.

programs.name: Business – Frequently Travelled Business Executives Visa (F4C)
programs.eligibility: High-profile business travelers who invest at least USD 250,000 in Nigeria. Requires proof of investment from a recognized agency, invitation letter, and CAC documentation for the host company.
programs.notes: Short Visit Visa (Embassy) valid for 1 year with multiple entries; each stay capped at 30 days. Employment not permitted. This is a short-stay visa category (not a residence permit). It is a prerequisite for applying for the Investor Visa Permanent Residence categories (N3A/N3B).

agreements: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) free movement of persons protocol

validation_notes: CORRECTION (2026-06-29): Rwanda (RWA) added to visa_free with max_stay_days=30. Nigeria Immigration Service Public Relations Officer Akinsola Akinlabi confirmed on ~May 15, 2026 that all implementation arrangements were completed across all international entry points (airports, land borders, seaports), following President Tinubu's announcement at the Africa CEO Forum in Kigali. Reciprocates Rwanda's existing visa waiver for Nigerian nationals. Source is NIS spokesman statement cited by multiple credible Nigerian outlets (Telegraph Nigeria, Vanguard, ThisDay, Guardian); no dedicated immigration.gov.ng announcement page found at time of June 2026 verification - recorded with source_official=false. Moved to gaps: specific passport types not delineated in official statement.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): NIS F5A page still states exemption for 'Citizens of all countries except ECOWAS member States and other countries which Nigeria has entered into visa abolition/waiver agreements' - ECOWAS exemption framework confirmed. Source: immigration.gov.ng/info-center/tourism-visa-f5a/.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): visa_on_arrival array remains empty - VoA abolished 1 May 2025; NIS visa classes page confirms e-visa only for short stays.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): e_visa max_stay_days = 90 confirmed - NIS visa classes page states 'not exceeding three months (90 days)' for short visit e-visas. Source: immigration.gov.ng/nigerian-visa/.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): Saint Kitts and Nevis visa-free (effective August 4, 2025) re-confirmed by official SKNIS page (sknis.gov.kn). Max stay of 90 days per NIS spokesperson Akinsola Akinlabi (TheCable); not stated on official SKN government announcement pages - retained in gaps.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): VON (von.gov.ng) Angola diplomatic/service/official passport waiver (signed Feb 15, 2026 at AU Summit, Addis Ababa) - content confirmed matches record.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): foreignaffairs.gov.ng page for Nigeria-Angola agreement still unreachable (ECONNREFUSED); agreement accepted as official based on .gov.ng domain in official search results and VON corroboration.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): interior.gov.ng/visa-policy/ confirms visa-agreement countries receive entry visas at points of entry; does not publish a named country list.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): Nigeria-UK agreements signed 2026 are MoU on Migration Partnership + Statements of Intent on Border Security and Business Visa Expansion for UK companies - NOT ordinary-passport visa-free entry; not added to visa_policy.

CORRECTION (2026-06-27): ECOWAS visa-free entry notes updated to reflect that Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger formally withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025 (one-year statutory notice period elapsed per Article 91 of the Revised ECOWAS Treaty). ECOWAS transition period ran January 29 – July 29, 2025, during which visa-free movement was explicitly maintained per ECOWAS Commission statements. After July 29, 2025, ECOWAS has not formally terminated visa-free movement for AES nationals; it continues 'until further notice'. The NIS official page (immigration.gov.ng/info-center/tourism-visa-f5a/) does not address AES country status. The nationality description in visa_free updated from listing 15 ECOWAS members to the 12 currently active members (Benin, Cabo Verde, Cote d'Ivoire, The Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo - plus Nigeria itself as host). BFA, MLI, NER post-withdrawal status added to data_quality.gaps.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): NIS Tourism Visa (F5A) page still states exemption for 'Citizens of all countries except ECOWAS member States and other countries which Nigeria has entered into visa abolition/waiver agreements' - ECOWAS exemption framework confirmed. Source: immigration.gov.ng/info-center/tourism-visa-f5a/.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): visa_on_arrival array remains empty - NIS visa classes page confirms e-visa only for short stays; VoA abolished 1 May 2025.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): e_visa max_stay_days = 90 confirmed - NIS visa classes page states 'not exceeding three months (90 days)' for short visit e-visas. Source: immigration.gov.ng/nigerian-visa/.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): Saint Kitts and Nevis visa-free (effective August 4, 2025) re-confirmed by official SKNIS page (sknis.gov.kn). Max stay of 90 days per NIS spokesperson Akinsola Akinlabi (TheCable); not stated on the official SKN government announcement pages - retained in gaps.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): VON (von.gov.ng) Angola diplomatic/service/official passport waiver (signed Feb 15, 2026 at AU Summit, Addis Ababa) - page accessible and content confirmed matches record.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): interior.gov.ng/visa-policy/ confirms visa-agreement countries receive entry visas at points of entry but does not publish a named country list.

CORRECTION: Saint Kitts and Nevis visa_free entry: source_official changed from false to true. The source URL (skncgtoronto.gov.kn) is the official Saint Kitts and Nevis Consulate General in Toronto - a .gov.kn domain, which is an official government domain.

CORRECTION: e_visa max_stay_days updated from 30 to 90. The NIS visa classes page explicitly states short visit visas allow stays 'not exceeding three months (90 days)'. Individual sub-categories (e.g., F5A Tourism = 30 days) have shorter specific limits; 90 is the stated ceiling. Notes updated to clarify both the ceiling and sub-category specifics.

CORRECTION: conditional_access entries now use iso3 arrays for eligible_nationalities (AGO, VNM, ETH) instead of the string 'any', which was incorrect - these are bilateral agreements covering specific nationalities only.

CORRECTION: ECOWAS status note updated: Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger are formally in withdrawal proceedings (ECOWAS extraordinary session 22–23 April 2025 in Accra) - they are no longer merely 'suspended', they are actively withdrawing. The ECOWAS free movement entry now reflects this distinction.

CORRECTION: Angola conditional_access source note updated to flag that the foreignaffairs.gov.ng page had an expired SSL certificate at time of June 2026 re-validation; the agreement itself is confirmed by the official MFA URL appearing in searches and corroborated by VON (state broadcaster).
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: visa_on_arrival array remains empty - VoA was phased out effective 1 May 2025, confirmed by NIS.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: cbi.has_program = false - no CBI program found on any official Nigerian government domain.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: rbi investor visa thresholds (N3A = USD 250k, N3B = USD 500k) confirmed consistent with official NIS info-center pages.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Tourism Visa F5A is 30-day stay (90-day validity from issuance) - confirmed by official NIS F5A page.
gaps: The specific named countries covered by Nigeria's bilateral visa abolition/waiver agreements (non-ECOWAS) for ordinary passport holders are not published in a consolidated official online list on immigration.gov.ng or interior.gov.ng.

Minimum investment thresholds for Investor Visas N3C (Large Scale Corporations), N3D (Ultra Large Scale Corporations), and N3E (Oil/Gas/Power Sector) are not stated as specific dollar amounts on the official info-center pages.
Minimum financial requirements for the Retirement Visa (N5B) are not specified as exact amounts on the official page.

Post-withdrawal visa-free status of Burkina Faso (BFA), Mali (MLI), and Niger (NER): These three countries formally withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025. ECOWAS maintained visa-free movement for their nationals during a six-month transition period (ends July 29, 2025) and has continued it 'until further notice' post-transition. Nigeria's official NIS website (immigration.gov.ng) does not address their current status - the F5A exemption page references only 'ECOWAS member States', which BFA/MLI/NER are no longer. Whether Nigeria individually extends visa-free access to these three nationalities (by bilateral agreement or extended ECOWAS-derived right) is not published on any official Nigerian government domain as of June 2026. Classified as unknown/unconfirmed; these nationalities are NOT listed in visa_free to avoid false visa-free error.
Path-to-citizenship timelines for investor or highly-skilled residence visa holders are not specified on official immigration.gov.ng pages.
No official digital nomad or startup visa program found on official Nigerian immigration sources.

Diplomatic/service passport waiver agreements with Brazil, China, Namibia, Singapore, South Africa, and Turkey are referenced in Wikipedia/TIMATIC but no standalone official Nigerian government (MFA/NIS) page was found to verify these; not recorded per official-source-first rules.

The Nigeria-Vietnam diplomatic/official passport waiver (signed Oct 2019, 90-day max stay) is confirmed by credible news sources but no standalone official foreignaffairs.gov.ng press-release page was found; recorded with source_official=false.

The Nigeria-Ethiopia diplomatic/official passport waiver (MOU signed Feb 2020, ratified by Ethiopia 2025) is confirmed by credible news sources but no standalone official foreignaffairs.gov.ng press-release page was found; recorded with source_official=false.

The foreignaffairs.gov.ng page for the Nigeria-Angola agreement had an expired SSL certificate at time of June 2026 re-validation; the agreement is accepted as official based on the .gov.ng domain appearing in official search results and VON corroboration.

No credential-based conditional entry rules found: Nigeria does not grant visa-free or e-visa access to foreign nationals solely on the basis of holding a valid US, UK, Schengen, Canadian, or other country's visa/residence permit.
No special-status conditional entry rules found: No OCI or similar diaspora/heritage status recognized by Nigeria for entry purposes.

Saint Kitts and Nevis visa-free agreement: specific passport types eligible (ordinary vs official/diplomatic) not delineated in the official SKNIS announcement texts. 90-day duration confirmed by NIS spokesperson Akinsola Akinlabi per TheCable.

Rwanda visa-free agreement (effective ~May 15, 2026): specific passport types eligible (ordinary vs official/diplomatic) not delineated in the official NIS spokesman statement. Recorded with source_official=false as no dedicated immigration.gov.ng page was found at time of June 2026 verification. The NIS F5A exemption page has not yet been updated to reflect Rwanda. Max stay of 30 days confirmed by NIS spokesman.

Nigeria-UK 2026 agreements: the Statements of Intent on Business Visa Expansion may lead to a future fast-track business visa arrangement for UK companies; current scope is not ordinary-passport visa-free. Monitor for further developments.

visa_types.name: Transit Without Visa (F3A)
visa_types.purpose: Allows airside transfer/transit passengers to connect to onward flights without entering Nigerian territory. Travelers must remain in the airport transit zone throughout.
visa_types.notes: No formal visa is issued. Maximum 48 hours in transit zone only. Requires confirmed onward ticket. Cannot be extended. Not applicable for those requiring entry into Nigeria.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa (F3B)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay transit visa for travelers passing through Nigeria who need to enter the country briefly before departing for a third destination.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal (evisa.immigration.gov.ng). Duration 1–7 days. Distinct from the airside Transit Without Visa (F3A).

visa_types.name: Tourism Visa (F5A)
visa_types.purpose: Single-entry short-stay visa granted to persons who wish to visit Nigeria for the purpose of tourism. Employment is not permitted.
visa_types.notes: Applies to non-ECOWAS nationals and citizens from countries without a Nigeria visa abolition/waiver agreement. Valid 90 days from issuance; maximum 30-day stay per visit; non-extendable. Group/family applications accepted (up to 10 persons). Requires valid passport (6+ months), return flight proof, hotel reservation or host address, and 180-day bank statement.

visa_types.name: Religious Tourism Visa (F7J)
visa_types.purpose: Single-entry visa for foreign nationals visiting Nigeria for religious tourism purposes such as pilgrimages or religious study tours.
visa_types.notes: Short-stay e-visa category. Employment not permitted.

visa_types.name: Medical Tourism Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals seeking medical treatment in Nigeria. Allows for an extended stay relative to standard short-stay visas.
visa_types.notes: Embassy/consulate visa. Entry validity up to 90 days; potential stay up to 180 days for ongoing medical treatment. Employment not permitted.

visa_types.name: Business Visa – Single Entry (F4A)
visa_types.purpose: Single-entry visa for foreign nationals visiting Nigeria for business purposes such as meetings, negotiations, or short-term commercial activities.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal. Employment not permitted. Requires invitation from a Nigerian-registered company.

visa_types.name: Business Visa – Multiple Entry (F4B)
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry short-stay visa for frequent business travelers visiting Nigeria on a reciprocal basis.
visa_types.notes: Issued on a reciprocal basis. Each visit capped at 30 days. Available via e-Visa portal.

visa_types.name: Business Visa – Frequently Travelled Executives (F4C)
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry business visa for high-profile executives who have invested at least USD 250,000 in Nigeria, allowing repeated short stays over 1 year.
visa_types.notes: Embassy visa. Requires proof of at least USD 250,000 invested in Nigeria (verified by a recognized agency), CAC documentation for host company, and invitation letter. Each stay capped at 30 days. This visa is also a prerequisite for applying for the Investor Permanent Residence Visa categories (N3A/N3B).

visa_types.name: Visiting Visa – Single Entry (F6A)
visa_types.purpose: Single-entry visa for foreign nationals visiting family or friends in Nigeria for personal/social purposes.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal. Employment not permitted.

visa_types.name: Visiting Visa – Multiple Entry
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry visa for foreign nationals making repeated visits to Nigeria for personal or family purposes, valid for up to 5 years.
visa_types.notes: Embassy visa. Validity up to 5 years; 180 days stay per visit. Employment not permitted.

visa_types.name: Sports Visa (F7E)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for foreign nationals visiting Nigeria to participate in sporting events, competitions, or sports-related activities.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal. Employment not permitted beyond the sporting event.

visa_types.name: Creative Arts Visa (F7F)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for foreign nationals visiting Nigeria for creative arts activities such as film, music, or cultural performances.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal.

visa_types.name: Study Tour Visa (F7G)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for foreign nationals visiting Nigeria for a structured educational study tour, not constituting full enrollment in an academic program.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal. Distinct from the full Student Visa (R7A/R7B).

visa_types.name: Academic Exchange Programme Visa – Short Stay (F7H)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for foreign academics or researchers participating in a specific academic exchange programme in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal. Duration up to 90 days single entry. Distinct from the long-stay Temporary Residence Academic Exchange visa (R8A/R8B).

visa_types.name: International Cultural Exchange Visa (F7I)
visa_types.purpose: Single-entry visa for foreign nationals participating in an officially recognized international cultural exchange programme in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal.

visa_types.name: Emergency/Relief Work Visa (F7K)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for foreign humanitarian or emergency relief workers deployed to Nigeria for disaster response or relief operations.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal.

visa_types.name: Journalist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreign journalists and media professionals visiting Nigeria for newsgathering, reporting, or media production purposes.
visa_types.notes: Embassy/consulate visa. Duration up to 90 days.

visa_types.name: Cleric Visa – Short Stay
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreign religious clerics visiting Nigeria for short-term pastoral, religious, or missionary activities.
visa_types.notes: Embassy/consulate visa. Entry validity up to 90 days; maximum stay once admitted is 30 days. Distinct from the long-stay Employment (Cleric) Visa (R6A–R6E).

visa_types.name: Humanitarian Services Visa
visa_types.purpose: Single-entry visa for foreign nationals providing humanitarian services in Nigeria through recognized non-governmental or international organizations.
visa_types.notes: Embassy/consulate visa.

visa_types.name: Staff of INGO Visa – Short Stay
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreign staff of International Non-Governmental Organizations (INGOs) visiting Nigeria for short-term programme activities.
visa_types.notes: Embassy/consulate visa. Duration up to 90 days single entry. Distinct from the long-stay Employment (INGO) Visa (R5A–R5E).

visa_types.name: Staff of NGO Visa – Short Stay
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreign staff of domestic Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) registered in Nigeria visiting for short-term engagements.
visa_types.notes: Embassy/consulate visa.

visa_types.name: Returning Holders of Foreign Passports – Nigerian by Birth (F7L)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay e-visa for persons born in Nigeria who now hold a foreign passport and wish to return for visits.
visa_types.notes: Available via e-Visa portal.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa – Non-Accredited (Embassy)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for diplomatic passport holders of countries that do not have an accreditation-based agreement with Nigeria, covering non-resident diplomatic travel.
visa_types.notes: Embassy/consulate visa. Duration up to 90 days. Distinct from the Accredited Diplomat Visa (R1A–R1E) for formally accredited diplomats.

visa_types.name: Accredited Diplomat Visa (R1A–R1E)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for formally accredited diplomatic and consular officers and their dependants posted to Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV). Valid for 2 years, renewable. Issued in conjunction with accreditation through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

visa_types.name: Employment (Expatriate) Visa (R2A–R2E)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for skilled foreign nationals employed by a company registered and operating in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Initial entry via a 90-day visa; holder then applies for the Combined Expatriate Residence Permit and Aliens Card (CERPAC) for a 2-year renewable residence/work permit. Employer must obtain a quota approval. Multiple sub-categories (R2A–R2E) based on position/seniority.

visa_types.name: Employment (Expatriate Free Zone) Visa (R3A–R3E)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for skilled foreign nationals employed within Nigeria's Export Processing Zones or Free Trade Zones.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV). Valid for 2 years, renewable. Sponsored by a Free Zone enterprise.

visa_types.name: Employment – Government Official (R4A–R4E)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for foreign nationals officially employed or seconded to a Nigerian government ministry, department, or agency.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV). Valid for 2 years, renewable.

visa_types.name: Employment (INGO) Visa (R5A–R5E)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for foreign nationals employed by an International Non-Governmental Organization registered and operating in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV). Valid for 2 years, renewable.

visa_types.name: Employment (Cleric) Visa (R6A–R6E)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for foreign religious clerics (pastors, imams, priests, etc.) taking up long-term pastoral positions in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV). Valid for 2 years, renewable. Distinct from the short-stay Cleric Visa.

visa_types.name: Temporary Work Permit – 90 Days (R10)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term work authorization for foreign nationals undertaking specific, time-limited work assignments in Nigeria not exceeding 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV) sub-category. Not renewable. Applied for via twp.immigration.gov.ng portal.

visa_types.name: Temporary Work Permit – 6 Months (R11)
visa_types.purpose: Medium-term work authorization for foreign nationals undertaking specific assignments in Nigeria for up to 6 months.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV) sub-category. Multiple entries; non-renewable. Applied for via twp.immigration.gov.ng portal.

visa_types.name: Student Visa (R7A/R7B)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for foreign nationals enrolled full-time in an accredited educational institution in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV). Initially issued for 1 year; renewable annually for the duration of the academic program. Requires acceptance letter from a recognized Nigerian institution.

visa_types.name: Academic Exchange Programme Visa – Long Stay (R8A/R8B)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for foreign academics, researchers, or professors participating in a long-term academic exchange programme at a Nigerian institution.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV) sub-category. Duration aligned with the exchange programme.

visa_types.name: Intern Visa (R9A)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary Residence Visa for foreign nationals undertaking internships or supervised training placements at Nigerian organizations.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Residence Visa (TRV) sub-category. Duration several months to 1 year; possible renewal depending on programme.

visa_types.name: Spouse of Nigerian Citizen Permanent Residence Visa (N1A)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for foreign spouses of Nigerian citizens who wish to reside permanently in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Requires evidence of valid marriage to a Nigerian citizen and spousal relationship documentation.

visa_types.name: Nigerian by Birth (Renounced Citizenship) Permanent Residence Visa (N2A/N2B)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for persons who were born Nigerian but have renounced their Nigerian citizenship, and their foreign spouses, wishing to reside in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). N2A for the person themselves; N2B for their foreign spouse.

visa_types.name: Investor Visa – Small Scale Enterprise (N3A)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for foreign investors who have imported and retained USD 250,000–500,000 as capital in a Nigerian small-scale enterprise.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Residence permit duration 3 years (multiple entry), subject to renewal. Requires prior holding of F4B or F4C visa, security clearance, proof of enterprise investment, and business plan.

visa_types.name: Investor Visa – Medium Scale Enterprise (N3B)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for foreign investors who have imported and retained USD 500,000–1,000,000 as capital in a Nigerian medium-scale enterprise.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Residence permit duration 4 years (multiple entry), subject to renewal. Requires prior F4B or F4C visa holding and security clearance.

visa_types.name: Investor Visa – Large Scale Corporations (N3C)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for senior executives of large corporations with significant capital and business presence in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Specific minimum capital threshold not stated verbatim on official page. Requires corporate registration and audited financial statements.

visa_types.name: Investor Visa – Ultra Large Scale Corporations (N3D)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for key executives of major multinational or ultra-large corporations with the highest levels of capital, assets, or turnover in Nigeria.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Specific minimum thresholds not stated verbatim on official page.

visa_types.name: Investor Visa – Oil, Gas & Power Sector (N3E)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for investors with significant ventures in Nigeria's oil, gas, or power sectors requiring specialized technical expertise and substantial capital.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Specific minimum capital not stated verbatim on official page. Requires industry licenses or partnerships.

visa_types.name: Highly Skilled Immigrant Visa (N4A)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for foreign nationals with specialized knowledge and technical expertise highly relevant to the current and future needs of the Nigerian economy.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Residence permit duration 5 years (multiple entry), extendable. Holder may work and reside in Nigeria. Requires recommendation letter from the Nigerian Skills Gap Assessment (under the Industrial Training Fund, ITF), vetted credentials, and police report.

visa_types.name: Retirement Visa – Retired in Nigeria (N5A)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for foreign nationals who have retired while residing in Nigeria and wish to continue residing there in retirement.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Employment not permitted. Specific financial threshold not stated verbatim on official page.

visa_types.name: Retirement Visa – Retiree from Abroad (N5B)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for foreign retirees wishing to relocate to Nigeria from overseas, demonstrating sufficient financial resources to support themselves.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Applicants must demonstrate sufficient financial resources (pensions, annuities, savings) and secure local accommodation. Health insurance strongly recommended. Employment not permitted. Specific minimum income/savings threshold not stated verbatim on official page.

visa_types.name: Spouse/Dependant of Permanent Resident Visa (N6A–N6D)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent Residence Visa for spouses and dependants of existing Nigerian Permanent Residence Visa holders.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Visa (PRV). Sub-categories N6A–N6D cover different classes of dependants. Employment restrictions may apply depending on sub-category.

outbound_official.note: No official Nigeria government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Nigeria Ministry of Foreign Affairs (foreignaffairs.gov.ng) Travel Advisory page gives only generic outbound tips ('Visa or Entry Permit (For Destination Country)') with no country-by-country list. The Nigeria Immigration Service (immigration.gov.ng) and its knowledge base are entirely inbound-focused. The MFA self-service portal (mfa.1gov.ng/selfservice) is a contact/tracking portal only. No dedicated outbound visa-free destination list for Nigerian ordinary-passport holders was found on any official Nigerian government domain (.gov.ng).

sources_checked.description: Nigeria Immigration Service – comprehensive visa classes page; confirms short-stay e-visa ceiling of 90 days and all visa categories
NIS Tourism Visa F5A – explicitly states ECOWAS members and visa-waiver agreement countries are exempt; confirms 30-day stay, 90-day validity from issuance
NIS Transit Without Visa F3A – airside transit up to 48 hours
NIS Short Visit e-Visa category index – lists all 13 e-visa sub-categories
NIS Investor Visa N3A – USD 250,000–500,000 minimum capital, 3-year residence permit
NIS Investor Visa N3B – USD 500,000–1,000,000 minimum capital, 4-year residence permit
NIS Highly Skilled Immigrant Visa N4A – 5-year residence permit for specialized experts
Federal Ministry of Interior – Nigeria Visa Policy page; confirms visa-agreement countries receive entry visas at points of entry
Nigeria Immigration Service – Official e-Visa portal (page body inaccessible at time of fetch)
ECOWAS – Extraordinary Council session 22–23 April 2025 on formal withdrawal of Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger

Nigerian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) Press Center – Official announcement of Nigeria-Angola diplomatic/official/service passport visa waiver agreement signed 15 February 2026; SSL certificate expired at time of June 2026 re-validation
Voice of Nigeria (official state broadcaster) – Nigeria-Angola visa waiver deal for diplomatic and official/service passport holders, 15 February 2026
Saint Kitts and Nevis Consulate General Toronto (official .gov.kn domain) – confirms visa-free access for SKN nationals to Nigeria effective 4 August 2025
Saint Kitts and Nevis Information Service (official government news service, .gov.kn domain) – same announcement on reciprocal visa-free access effective 4 August 2025
Channels Television – Nigeria-Vietnam visa waiver agreement for diplomatic and official passport holders, signed 30 October 2019 at Presidential Villa
TheCable – Nigeria-Ethiopia visa waiver agreement for diplomatic passport holders, signed February 2020
TheCable – NIS spokesperson Akinsola Akinlabi confirms Nigeria-Saint Kitts and Nevis 90-day reciprocal visa-free agreement
Wikipedia – Visa policy of Nigeria (aggregator; lists Brazil/China/Namibia/Singapore/South Africa/Turkey diplomatic passport waivers citing TIMATIC – not used as official source)
NIS Info Center index – full list of PRV, TRV, and short-stay visa sub-categories with official codes (R and N series)
Federal Ministry of Interior – Nigeria-UK agreements on Migration Partnership, Border Security, and Business Visa Expansion (May 2026); NOT ordinary-passport visa-free entry

Telegraph Nigeria – NIS spokesman Akinsola Akinlabi confirms 30-day visa-free entry for Rwandan nationals effective ~May 15, 2026; reciprocal arrangement announced by President Tinubu at Africa CEO Forum in Kigali
Vanguard Nigeria – corroborates NIS spokesman statement on 30-day visa-free entry for Rwandan nationals (May 2026)

NIS News Archive – June 2026 news items reviewed; no new bilateral visa-free agreement announcements found beyond Rwanda; recent news covers officer development, cybersecurity, and youth engagement programs
